title: Larry Harris
text: Home * People * Larry Harris Larry Harris [1] Larry R. Harris , an American computer scientist and retired commercial AI entrepreneur. He holds a Ph.D. in computer science in 1972 from Cornell University . Contents 1 Computer Chess 2 AI Entrepreneur 3 Selected Publications 4 External Links 5 References Computer Chess [ edit ] During the 70s, as professor of computer science at Dartmouth College , Larry Harris researched and published on heuristic best-first search based on A* and introduced the Bandwidth Search [2] . He lead the team developing Dartmouth CP and Dart [3] , competing at the ACM 1973 and ACM 1974 respectively. AI Entrepreneur [ edit ] Larry Harris further research interests were related to natural language query systems , and he founded Artificial Intelligence Corporation , later renamed AICorp , which went public in 1990. In 1994 he founded the EasyAsk [4] Linguistic Technology Corporation, and developed its EasyAsk and English Wizard E-commerce products. Selected Publications [ edit ] [5] Larry Harris ( 1973 ). The bandwidth heuristic search . 3. IJCAI 1973 , pdf Larry Harris ( 1974 ). Heuristic Search under Conditions of Error . Artificial Intelligence , Vol. 5, No. 3, also as ( 1977 ). The heuristic search: An alternative to the alpha-beta minimax procedure. Chess Skill in Man and Machine Larry Harris ( 1975 ). The Heuristic Search And The Game Of Chess - A Study Of Quiescence, Sacrifices, And Plan Oriented Play . 4. IJCAI 1975 , reprinted ( 1988 ) in Computer Chess Compendium Larry Harris ( 1977 ). The heuristic search: An alternative to the alpha-beta minimax procedure.
